faqs.question: What is a CPU?
faqs.answer: A CPU (Central Processing Unit) is the brain of your PC. It handles all the instructions from programs. Popular choices are Intel Core i5/i7 and AMD Ryzen 5/7.

faqs.question: What is a GPU?
faqs.answer: A GPU (Graphics Processing Unit) handles graphics and gaming. For gaming, consider NVIDIA RTX 3060 or AMD RX 6600 as mid-range options.

faqs.question: How much RAM do I need for gaming?
faqs.answer: 16GB RAM is the sweet spot for gaming in 2024. 8GB is the minimum, and 32GB is ideal for gaming and streaming simultaneously.

faqs.question: What is a motherboard?
faqs.answer: A motherboard is the main circuit board that connects all PC components together. Make sure it's compatible with your CPU socket type.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Intel and AMD?
faqs.answer: Intel excels in single-core performance and is great for gaming. AMD offers better multi-core performance and value for money, ideal for content creation.

faqs.question: What power supply do I need?
faqs.answer: Calculate your total system power consumption and add 20% headroom. A 650W PSU is good for most gaming builds. Always choose 80+ Gold certified PSUs.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SSD and HDD?
faqs.answer: SSDs are much faster than HDDs and are ideal for your OS and games. HDDs are cheaper and better for mass storage. A combination of both is recommended.

faqs.question: What is a CPU cooler?
faqs.answer: A CPU cooler prevents your processor from overheating. Stock coolers work for basic use, but for gaming or overclocking, get an aftermarket air or liquid cooler.

faqs.question: What is thermal paste?
faqs.answer: Thermal paste fills microscopic gaps between your CPU and cooler, improving heat transfer. Apply a pea-sized amount in the center of the CPU.

faqs.question: What PC case should I buy?
faqs.answer: Choose a case based on your motherboard size (ATX, Micro-ATX, Mini-ITX), airflow quality, and cable management options. Make sure all components fit inside.

faqs.question: What is overclocking?
faqs.answer: Overclocking means running your CPU or GPU faster than its default speed. It improves performance but generates more heat and requires better cooling.

faqs.question: What is the difference between ATX and Micro-ATX motherboards?
faqs.answer: ATX motherboards are larger with more expansion slots. Micro-ATX are smaller and cheaper with fewer slots. Choose based on your case size and expansion needs.

faqs.question: How do I choose a gaming monitor?
faqs.answer: For gaming, look for at least 144Hz refresh rate, 1ms response time, and 1080p or 1440p resolution. IPS panels offer better colors while TN panels are faster.

faqs.question: What is RAM speed and does it matter?
faqs.answer: RAM speed is measured in MHz. Faster RAM improves performance especially with AMD Ryzen CPUs. DDR4 3200MHz is the sweet spot for most gaming builds.

faqs.question: How do I build a budget gaming PC?
faqs.answer: For a budget build, consider AMD Ryzen 5 CPU, 16GB DDR4 RAM, NVIDIA RTX 3060 or AMD RX 6600 GPU, 500GB SSD, and a B550 motherboard. Total budget around $600-800.
